questions.questionText: Which vitamin deficiency causes night blindness and xerophthalmia?
questions.explanation: Retinal (Vitamin A) is a component of rhodopsin in rod cells.

questions.questionText: Wernicke-Korsakoff syndrome is seen in chronic alcoholics due to a deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Thiamine deficiency impairs transketolase and PDH complex in the brain.

questions.questionText: Pellagra is characterized by the '3 Ds' (Dermatitis, Diarrhea, Dementia) and is caused by deficiency of:
questions.explanation: Niacin deficiency causes Pellagra. Niacin can be synthesized from tryptophan.

questions.questionText: Which vitamin is required as a cofactor for the hydroxylation of proline and lysine in collagen synthesis?
questions.explanation: Scurvy results from vitamin C deficiency, leading to weak collagen and bleeding gums.

questions.questionText: Pernicious anemia is caused by impaired absorption of which vitamin due to lack of intrinsic factor?
questions.explanation: Autoimmune destruction of parietal cells leads to a lack of intrinsic factor.

questions.questionText: Which vitamin functions as a lipid-soluble antioxidant in cell membranes?
questions.explanation: Vitamin E (Tocopherol) protects polyunsaturated fatty acids from oxidative damage.

questions.questionText: Folic acid deficiency during early pregnancy is strongly associated with:
questions.explanation: Folate is required for nucleotide synthesis during rapid cell division in the embryo.

questions.questionText: Which mineral is a critical component of glutathione peroxidase, an antioxidant enzyme?
questions.explanation: Selenium is incorporated as selenocysteine in glutathione peroxidase.

questions.questionText: Wilson's disease involves the toxic accumulation of which mineral in the liver and brain?
questions.explanation: Defect in the ATP7B gene impairs biliary copper excretion.

questions.questionText: Hemochromatosis is an inherited disorder characterized by excessive intestinal absorption of:
questions.explanation: Excessive iron deposits in tissues (liver, heart, pancreas) causing damage.

questions.questionText: In Vitamins and Minerals, which statement is most accurate regarding the core definition and diagnostic concept?
questions.optionA: It reflects the most exam-relevant definition used in standard Biochemistry texts
questions.optionB: It is an obsolete definition no longer used in clinical practice
questions.optionC: It is used only in pediatric population and never in adults
questions.optionD: It has no diagnostic significance in NEET-PG pattern questions
questions.explanation: This tests foundational definition-level knowledge from standard Biochemistry references for Vitamins and Minerals. Subject: Biochemistry. Chapter: Vitamins and Minerals.

questions.questionText: The most important etiology/risk factor to suspect first in a typical Vitamins and Minerals scenario is:
questions.optionA: The common high-yield risk factor repeatedly asked in entrance exams
questions.optionB: A rare cause seen only in isolated case reports
questions.optionD: An etiology excluded by routine first-line workup
questions.explanation: NEET-PG and INI-CET focus on common, high-yield causes before rare differentials. Subject: Biochemistry. Chapter: Vitamins and Minerals.
